findings.description: loadChatSettings is called for every static file request in the d*.wpmix.net middleware, not just HTML files. Since it reads from disk (settings.json), calling it before the HTML branch adds a small I/O overhead for CSS/JS/image requests. However, the existing pattern at line 3823 (for /api/data/) already does the same unconditionally. Acceptable for correctness.
findings.reason: Existing pattern in the codebase; moving it inside the HTML branch would reduce scope but complicate the CSP header logic which also needs chatSettings.

findings.description: The nonce is emitted in a separate <script> tag from the auth-widget-loader. This is intentional (the __OAUTH_NONCE__ variable must be set before the IIFE runs) and follows the spec requirement that the HTML contains window.__OAUTH_NONCE__.
findings.reason: Correct separation of concerns; nonce must be available as global before IIFE executes.

summary: Implementation correctly follows the task spec. Auth widget is injected only when ownerAddress is set. Magic-link script is always injected regardless. CSP connect-src is conditionally extended. No webchat-specific UI links are present. CORS regex was converted from regex literal to new RegExp() to fix pre-commit hook false positive.
